verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (human kallikrein-2, hK2) is a serine protease secreted via the classical secretory pathway with a signal peptide. It is a secreted protein, but it is well-documented to remain transiently cell-surface associated on prostatic epithelial cells and prostate cancer cells, where it binds to cell-surface receptors (e.g., LRP) and extracellular matrix components. However, more importantly, KLK2 is a secreted protein without a TM domain or GPI anchor, and its surface association is reversible/non-covalent. Applying the recruitment and wash tests, it would wash off. The dominant biology is secreted. Verdict: secreted_only / no.
